lesson: Define __eq__ comparing a tuple of fields and __hash__ returning hash of that same tuple so instances that are == also collapse correctly in sets and dict keys.
Back a @property with a private _attr and validate in the setter; assigning through the property (even in __init__) enforces the invariant everywhere.
Use dataclasses.field(default_factory=list) for mutable defaults so each instance gets its own list, then use __post_init__ to validate/normalize constructor input.
In cooperative multiple inheritance, the order of base classes in the class statement changes what super() resolves to at each step -- check __mro__, don't guess.
Use abc.ABC with @abstractmethod to make a class uninstantiable until subclassed, then favor composition (store a strategy object) over inheriting from it so behavior can be swapped at runtime.
Every subclass of a __slots__ class must redeclare __slots__ (even empty) or it silently regains a __dict__ and loses the memory/attribute-restriction benefit.

In a diamond hierarchy, every override must call super().method() cooperatively -- C3 linearization (__mro__) guarantees each class runs once, but skipping one super() call breaks the chain and silently drops remaining classes.

When a dataclass subclass adds a required field after a parent field with a default, mark the new field field(kw_only=True) (3.10+) instead of reordering, since kw-only fields are exempt from the positional default-ordering check.
